w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: The citizen opens the Sadak-Sevak mobile application after noticing:
Step 4 — Data Upload to Cloud Server
The mobile app sends data to the cloud server.
The backend stores data in the database.
The complaint post becomes visible to all users.
All complaints appear on a live map.
This creates a community road awareness system.
This increases prediction accuracy over time.
User → Upload Image → GPS Capture → Cloud Server → AI Analysis → Public Feed → Government Dashboard → Repair Team → Status Update → Citizen Verification
After repair completion, the app waits 30 days
Users are asked whether the road is properly repaired
post is archived/removed from active map
If the local government department does not take action within a certain time, the complaint automatically moves to a higher authority.
Yes — that is actually a much better and more modern idea.
This will make your app look more advanced and unique.
AI scans the image and creates:
